summary: Hypercalciuria distinguishes Bartter syndrome from Gitelman syndrome, making it central to diagnosis despite normal renal function markers like renin levels. All presented symptoms—polyuria, polydipsia, fatigue, hypokalemia—and laboratory features support Bartter syndrome as the correct diagnosis.
